steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content: ”They are hardly pristine” is an independent clause. The word “though” is a subordinating conjunction, making any clause it introduces dependent on the first clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for punctuating subordinating conjunctions
steps.content:
The keyed punctuation is choice B (“pristine, though;”): the comma after “pristine” separates the dependent connector “though”, and the semicolon then breaks before the next clause.

Choice A uses a semicolon before “though”, choice C adds an unnecessary comma after “though”, and choice D omits the semicolon needed before the next clause.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
